---
title: "STA106_Midterm2_Project_RCode"
author: "Samuel Huang"
date: "2026-02-27"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra) 
library(car)      
library(EnvStats) 
library(emmeans)
library(DescTools)
```

# Load the data
```{r}
df <- read.csv("poisons.csv")

# poison and treatment are factors
df$poison <- factor(df$poison)
df$treat <- factor(df$treat)
```

\newpage

# Figures
```{r}
# Figure 1: histogram, survivial vs frequency
ggplot(df, aes(x = time)) +
    geom_histogram(color =  "black",fill="skyblue", binwidth = 0.082) +
    ggtitle("Distribution of Survival Time for all data samples") +
    xlab("Survival Time") +
    ylab("Frequency")

# Figure 2: Boxplot, survivial vs poison
ggplot(df, aes(x=poison, y=time, fill=poison)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle("Survival Time by Poison Type") +
  xlab("Poison Type") +
  ylab("Survival Time")  

# Figure 3: boxplot, survival vs treatment 
ggplot(df, aes(x=treat, y=time, fill=treat)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Survival Time by Different Treatment Type",
       x = "Treatment Type",
       y = "Survival Time")

# Figure 4: survival time vs treatment, by poison type 
ggplot(df, aes(x=treat, y=time, fill=poison)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Survival Time by Different Treatment and Poison Type",
       x = "Treatment Type",
       y = "Survival Time")

```

\newpage

# Summaries
```{r}
# 1. Grouped Summary (Poison & Treatment)
summary_stats <- df %>%
  group_by(poison, treat) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2. Summary by Poison Type
poison_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(poison) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. Summary by Treatment Type
treat_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(treat) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 4. Overall Summary  
overall_stats <- df %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )
```

\newpage

# Tables
```{r}
# Table 1 group summary, poison and treatment types
group_stats <- df %>%
  group_by(poison, treat) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time),
    SD_Time = sd(time),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(
  group_stats,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Summary Statistic by Poison Types and Treatment Types",
  col.names = c("Poison", "Treatment", "Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample size")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# Table2,  summary stat, poison type
p.means <- by(df$time, df$poison, mean)
p.sds   <- by(df$time, df$poison, sd)
p.nis   <- by(df$time, df$poison, length)
poison_table <- rbind(p.means, p.sds, p.nis)
rownames(poison_table) <- c("Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample size")

knitr::kable(
  t(poison_table),
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Summary Statistic by Poison Type",
  col.names = c("Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample size")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# Table3, summary stat, by treatment type
t.means <- by(df$time, df$treat, mean)
t.sds   <- by(df$time, df$treat, sd)
t.nis   <- by(df$time, df$treat, length)
treat_table <- rbind(t.means, t.sds, t.nis)
rownames(treat_table) <- c("Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample size")

knitr::kable(
  t(treat_table),
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Summary Statistic by Treatment Types",
  col.names = c("Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample size")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)
```

# Diagnosistic
```{r}

# fit initial model
model_initial <- lm(time ~ poison * treat, data = df)

# 1. Outlier Check with Studentized Residuals and Bonferroni cutoff
nt <- nrow(df)
a <- length(unique(df$poison))
b <- length(unique(df$treat))
df_resid <- nt - (a * b)
alpha <- 0.05
t.cutoff <- qt(1 - alpha / (2 * nt), df_resid) 
cat("cutoff value = ", t.cutoff, "\n")

# Outlier via semi-studentized/standardized residuals
MSE <- sum(model_initial$residuals^2) / df_resid
semi_studentized <- model_initial$residuals / sqrt(MSE)
outliers_semi <- which(abs(semi_studentized) > t.cutoff)
cat("Outliers via Semi-studentized residuals:", paste(outliers_semi, collapse=", "), "\n")

# Outliers via studentized/standardized residuals (rstandard)
rij <- rstandard(model_initial)
outliers_std <- which(abs(rij) > t.cutoff)
cat("Outliers via studentized residuals (rstandard):", paste(outliers_std, collapse=", "), "\n\n")


# 2. Normality Check
car::qqPlot(model_initial$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ Plot: Initial Model")
shapiro_initial <- shapiro.test(model_initial$residuals)
print(shapiro_initial)

# 3. Constant Variance Check
plot(model_initial, which = 3, main = "Residual v.s. Fitted: Initial Model")
levene_initial <- leveneTest(model_initial)
print(levene_initial)

# 4. Evalutate Two Transformations
# Test Box-Cox Transformation
# get best lambda using Shapiro-Wilk
L2 <- boxcox(model_initial, objective.name = "Shapiro-Wilk", optimize = TRUE)$lambda

# apply transformation
df$time_box_trans <- (df$time^L2 - 1) / L2

# fit new model with transformed data
model_box_trans <- lm(time_box_trans ~ poison * treat, data = df)

# redo diagnostics on transformed model
car::qqPlot(model_box_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ Plot: Box-cox Transformed Model")
print(shapiro.test(model_box_trans$residuals))
plot(model_box_trans, which = 3, main = "Residual v.s. Fitted: Box-cox Transformed Model")
print(leveneTest(model_box_trans))


# Test Log Transformation
L3 <- boxcox(df$time, objective.name = "Log-Likelihood", optimize = TRUE)$lambda
df$time_log_trans <- (df$time^L3 - 1) / L3
model_log_trans <- lm(time_log_trans ~ poison * treat, data = df)

car::qqPlot(model_log_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ Plot: Log Transformed Model")
print(shapiro.test(model_log_trans$residuals))
plot(model_log_trans, which = 3, main = "Residual v.s. Fitted: Log Transformed Model")
print(leveneTest(model_log_trans))


# Test PPCC Transformation
L4 <- boxcox(model_initial, objective.name = "PPCC", optimize = TRUE)$lambda
df$time_ppcc_trans <- (df$time^L4 - 1) / L4
model_ppcc_trans <- lm(time_ppcc_trans ~ poison * treat, data = df)

car::qqPlot(model_ppcc_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ Plot: PPCC Transformed Model")
print(shapiro.test(model_ppcc_trans$residuals))
plot(model_ppcc_trans, which = 3, main = "Residual v.s. Fitted: PPCC Transformed Model")
print(leveneTest(model_ppcc_trans))

# p-value comparison table
p_shap_initial <- shapiro.test(model_initial$residuals)$p.value
p_shap_ppcc <- shapiro.test(model_ppcc_trans$residuals)$p.value
p_shap_sw <- shapiro.test(model_box_trans$residuals)$p.value
p_shap_ll <- shapiro.test(model_log_trans$residuals)$p.value

p_lev_initial <- leveneTest(model_initial)$"Pr(>F)"[1]
p_lev_ppcc <- leveneTest(model_ppcc_trans)$"Pr(>F)"[1]
p_lev_sw <- leveneTest(model_box_trans)$"Pr(>F)"[1]
p_lev_ll <- leveneTest(model_log_trans)$"Pr(>F)"[1]

diagnostic_summary <- data.frame(
  Transformation = c(
    "None (Initial Model)", 
    "PPCC Box-Cox", 
    "Shapiro-Wilk Box-Cox", 
    "Log-Likelihood"
  ),
  Normality_P = c(p_shap_initial, p_shap_ppcc, p_shap_sw, p_shap_ll),
  Variance_P = c(p_lev_initial, p_lev_ppcc, p_lev_sw, p_lev_ll)
)

knitr::kable(
  diagnostic_summary,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Comparison of Test P-values Across Transformations",
  col.names = c("Transformation Method", "Shapiro-Wilk P-value", "Brown-Forsythe P-value")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# select final model based on diagnostics
final_model <- model_log_trans

# 5. Model Selection and Interaction Hypothesis Test

# interaction plot with full model (model_log_trans)
emmip(model_log_trans, poison ~ treat) +
  labs(
    title = "Interaction Plot of Log-Survival Time",
    x = "Treatment Type",
    y = "Estimated Marginal Mean (Log of Time)",
    color = "Poison Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# fit the reduced model (main effects only, no interaction) 
model_no_interaction <- lm(time_log_trans ~ poison + treat, data = df)

# ANOVA test to compare the reduced model to the full model
interaction_test <- anova(model_no_interaction, model_log_trans)
knitr::kable(
  interaction_test,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Hypothesis Test for Interaction (Reduced vs. Full Model)",
  col.names = c("Res.Df", "RSS", "Df", "Sum of Sq", "F value", "Pr(>F)")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# Partial R square value for interaction: R^2(AB | A+B)
anova_full_model <- anova(model_log_trans)
SS_interaction <- anova_full_model["poison:treat", "Sum Sq"]
SS_error_full <- anova_full_model["Residuals", "Sum Sq"]

partial_R2_interaction <- SS_interaction / (SS_interaction + SS_error_full)

cat("\n", "Partial R^2 (AB|A+B) for the Interaction Term: ", round(partial_R2_interaction, 4), "\n")

# select final model without interaction as we failed to reject interaction hypothesis
final_additive_model <- aov(time_log_trans ~ poison + treat, data = df)
```

# 2x2 grid plot graph
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# combine qq plots
par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) 
car::qqPlot(model_initial$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ: Initial")
car::qqPlot(model_ppcc_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ: PPCC")
car::qqPlot(model_box_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ: Shapiro-Wilk")
car::qqPlot(model_log_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ: Log")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) 

# combine res vs fit plots
par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) 
plot(model_initial, which = 3, main = "Res vs Fit: Initial")
plot(model_ppcc_trans, which = 3, main = "Res vs Fit: PPCC")
plot(model_box_trans, which = 3, main = "Res vs Fit: Shapiro-Wilk")
plot(model_log_trans, which = 3, main = "Res vs Fit: Log")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```
\newpage

# Analysis
```{r}
# 1. ANOVA Table
anova_results <- anova(final_additive_model)
knitr::kable(
  anova_results,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Two-Way ANOVA Table for Final Additive Model"
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# main effects partial R square R^2(A|B) and R^2(B|A)
SS_poison <- anova_results["poison", "Sum Sq"]
SS_treat <- anova_results["treat", "Sum Sq"]
SS_error_add <- anova_results["Residuals", "Sum Sq"]

partial_R2_poison <- SS_poison / (SS_poison + SS_error_add)
partial_R2_treat <- SS_treat / (SS_treat + SS_error_add)

cat("Partial R^2 (A|B) for Factor A (Poison):", round(partial_R2_poison, 4), "\n")
cat("Partial R^2 (B|A) for Factor B (Treatment):", round(partial_R2_treat, 4), "\n")

# 2. Confidence Intervals (4 Pairwise, 2 Contrasts)

# --- 4 Pairwise ---
# all pairwise CI
scheffe_pairwise <- ScheffeTest(final_additive_model, conf.level = 0.95)
scheffe_poison_df <- as.data.frame(scheffe_pairwise$poison)
scheffe_treat_df <- as.data.frame(scheffe_pairwise$treat)
scheffe_poison_df$Pair <- rownames(scheffe_poison_df)
scheffe_treat_df$Pair <- rownames(scheffe_treat_df)
combined_scheffe_pairwise <- rbind(scheffe_poison_df, scheffe_treat_df)
combined_scheffe_pairwise <- combined_scheffe_pairwise[, c("Pair", "diff", "lwr.ci", "upr.ci", "pval")]
combined_scheffe_pairwise$pval <- format.pval(combined_scheffe_pairwise$pval, eps = 0.0001, digits = 4)
knitr::kable(
  combined_scheffe_pairwise,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  row.names = FALSE,
  caption = "All Pairwise Comparisons for Main Effects (Scheffe)",
  col.names = c("Contrast Pair", "Difference", "Lower CI", "Upper CI", "P-Value")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"))

# --- 2 Contrasts (not pairwise) ---
# contrast 1: Poison 1 vs average of Poison 2 and 3 (across all treatments)
# contrast 2: Average of Treat A & C vs Average of Treat B & D (across all poisons)
emm_poison <- emmeans(final_additive_model, ~ poison)
emm_treat <- emmeans(final_additive_model, ~ treat)

contrast_list_poison <- list("Poison 1 vs Poison (2+3)/2" = c(1, -0.5, -0.5))
contrast_list_treat <- list("Treat (A+C)/2 vs Treat (B+D)/2" = c(0.5, -0.5, 0.5, -0.5))

c_poison <- summary(contrast(emm_poison, contrast_list_poison, adjust = "scheffe"), infer = c(TRUE, TRUE))
c_treat <- summary(contrast(emm_treat, contrast_list_treat, adjust = "scheffe"), infer = c(TRUE, TRUE))

combined_custom <- rbind(as.data.frame(c_poison), as.data.frame(c_treat))

formatted_custom_contrasts <- data.frame(
  Contrast = combined_custom$contrast,
  diff = combined_custom$estimate,
  lwr.ci = combined_custom$lower.CL,
  upr.ci = combined_custom$upper.CL,
  pval = format.pval(combined_custom$p.value, eps = 0.0001, digits = 4)
)

knitr::kable(
  formatted_custom_contrasts,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  row.names = FALSE,
  caption = "Custom Non-Pairwise Contrasts (Scheffe)",
  col.names = c("Contrast", "Difference", "Lower CI", "Upper CI", "P-Value")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)
```
